articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (VfL Wolfsburg vs. Borussia Dortmund - Game Highlights) 2024/02/17 ESHEET

article.story:
Borussia Dortmund and hosts VfL Wolfsburg shared the points in a 1-1 draw on Saturday, leaving the visitors fourth in the Bundesliga, five behind VfB Stuttgart in third.

Dortmund grabbed the lead when Wolfsburg defenders failed to clear a Marco Reus shot and keeper Koen Casteels pushed it into the path of Niclas Füllkrug who stabbed the ball over the line.

Wolfsburg's Kevin Behrens had two chances in first-half stoppage time, including a shot that hit the woodwork but Yannick Gerhardt did better in the 64th minute, nodding in from close range to level.

The game was repeatedly interrupted when spectators threw tennis balls onto the pitch in an ongoing fan protest over a planned foreign investment in the Bundesliga.

Dortmund are on 41 points, four ahead of RB Leipzig in fifth, and five behind Stuttgart, 2-1 winners at Darmstadt 98 despite playing with 10 men for the entire second half.

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Yannick Gerhardt scores goal for VfL Wolfsburg) 2024/02/17 ESHEET

keyEvents.text: Goal!  VfL Wolfsburg 0, Borussia Dortmund 1. Niclas Füllkrug (Borussia Dortmund) left footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Yannick Gerhardt replaces Mattias Svanberg.
Second Half begins VfL Wolfsburg 0, Borussia Dortmund 1.
Goal!  VfL Wolfsburg 1, Borussia Dortmund 1. Yannick Gerhardt (VfL Wolfsburg) header from very close range to the high centre of the goal    following a corner.
Yannick Gerhardt (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Lukas Nmecha replaces Kevin Behrens.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Salih Özcan replaces Marco Reus.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Jamie Bynoe-Gittens replaces Jadon Sancho.
Substitution, Borussia Dortmund. Youssoufa Moukoko replaces Niclas Füllkrug.
Substitution, VfL Wolfsburg. Amin Sarr replaces Jonas Wind.
Maximilian Arnold (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card.
Moritz Jenz (VfL Wolfsburg) is shown the yellow card.
Youssoufa Moukoko (Borussia Dortmund) is shown the yellow card.
Second Half ends, VfL Wolfsburg 1, Borussia Dortmund 1.
